intro: Irregular future verbs keep the regular future endings, but use a shortened or changed stem. These forms are common with everyday verbs like tener, poder, hacer, decir, venir, salir, poner, and querer.

overview.body: Irregular future verbs keep the regular future endings, but use a shortened or changed stem. These forms are common with everyday verbs like tener, poder, hacer, decir, venir, salir, poner, and querer. Focus on the message first, then choose the form that fits the context.

examples.english: Future irregular stems: another useful example.

uses.heading: Real use
uses.body: Use this pattern when future irregular stems helps express a real message, not just a grammar label.

uses.heading: Common context
uses.body: This form often appears in everyday explanations, plans, advice, and reactions depending on the sentence.
